park_name: Port Vincent Caravan Park and Seaside Cabins

reviews.review_text: Caravan park up for sale. Not children or pet friendly. Port Vincent is worth while visiting.

"Buyer beware" and check where your caravan site will be.  There is a colour bond fenced off section of land adjacent to the office which is located on the opposite side of the road to the ocean and is in complete contrast to the coastal experience of being waterside, instead leaving you feel quite hemmed in.  One third of this fenced area was roped off when we arrived and we were instructed to keep off that land so the grass could grow, even though that was our allocated site.  With 7 x 21ft vans in our group (booked 2 months prior to our stay), the only way we would have fitted into this space was to manoeuvre 1 van at a time into position and have all vehicles parked out on the street.  The limited room would not allow everyone to open out their awning.  This was unsuitable and management agreed, thereby offering us a refund, despite the threat of the male manager of the park yelling at us, "you won't be getting your money back!"
We gratefully accepted the offer of a refund and moved on to enjoy our adventures elsewhere.

We stayed in a deluxe cabin which was modern and spotlessly clean. The lady at reception was pleasant and helpful. Lovely grassed sites for caravans. Park is well maintained, however the amenities and camp kitchen could do with some renovations. Overall, we enjoyed our stay.
Needed accomodation in an emergency, went to park at 11 o,clock but reception closed manager was walking by but abruptly said come back at 2pm and was  ot prepared to

The Caravan Park  accommodation was not as advertised — several key features shown in the listing were significantly different upon arrival, which was really disappointing.
What made the experience worse was the attitude of management. When we raised our concerns, they were dismissive and came across as quite rude, with little effort made to resolve the issues however we where offered refund, then when we drove away the Male Manger yelled at us that "we will not be getting refund"
Overall, this fell short of basic customer service standards. I would recommend others double-check details before booking, as our experience was far from what was promised.
We spent a very relaxing 2 days at this park. The owner was very welcoming and friendly. Our cabin was spacious and well appointed. The park was neat tidy and quiet. Recommended.

We recently stayed here, the sites are small, yet very grassy and it’s close the the beach, so that’s the positives. The manager (Mark) is rude and obnoxious, just not meant to be in the hospitality industry..  The park is for sale, which is not a surprise, as he  does not want to be there and does not want you to be  there either… Do not book here, go to the other van park in town or stay somewhere else…
Needed towels and bedding 🤔

Stunning location IF NO WIND.  Cabin nice but had musty smell upon entering. Good air con and TV. Minimal toiletries for 4 day / night stay. Vertical blinds at front missing a slat and no connecting chains. Overall fairly comfortable and just needs a coat of paint.

We have stayed here 4 or 5 times over the years. The budget accommodation is exactly that. The newer cabins are nicely fitted out and the park is cleaned each day. The drive through powered sites are average in size, so a bit squeezy if you have a larger van. This trip however I made an error of judgment in cleaning my car. I was told not to use a hose as the water is expensive, so I stopped. However, later in the day I sprayed the roof only and was berated by the foul-mouthed owner. I admit I did the wrong thing but the tirade that followed was full of expletives, and I was accused of being a rich entitled a-hole and was told to leave the park immediately. He then walked off. I admit I was wrong and tried to apologize but he wasn't having it. I deserved a stern talking to I agree, but not this. After a while I went to the office to try and apologize again and was told to f**k off.||Not many visitors there this week, only about 6-8 guests, mainly in the cabins, yet the foreshore caravan park was almost full. After reading other reviews I can see why. The park has been up for sale for the last year or so. It's time for a new owner as the current staff clearly have had enough, not even a hello from either of them during the stay to anyone. We won't be returning unless there is a change of ownership. The park responses to previous negative reviews ignore the bad feedback and talk about the team. It's a team of two (the owners I believe).

Very tired...games room not available.
Limited facilities
Staff not really friendly.

Couldn't recommend

Got here for a two night stay today. 28/01.
Two bed room cabin near the entrance.
Nice clean and tidy with comfy bed and nice space on the living area.
The grounds around the site are excellent.
Great view of the gulf waters.
Rated it 4 stars
The lady in reception very welcoming and friendy.
Have long history visiting Port Vincent great seaside town on Yorkes.

Thank you for having us.
Cheers
Maggie and Steve
Adelaide.😁
Spent a great 4 nights at the park in our van over xmas, owners Mark & Sharon where great and hospitable, ammenites nice and clean, all in all would definitely recommend..

We stayed at the caravan park in Port Vincent with my family, including our nana and pop, who brought their dog along. Although it’s supposed to be a dog-friendly park, we had a really unpleasant experience with an old grumpy man who kept hassling them about their dog barking. He was very rude and even threatened to make them leave without a refund, which was totally unfair. He doesn’t even greet people—not even a hello—just a grumpy look up and down if that. This ruined our yearly holiday and forced us to cut it short and book somewhere else. Everyone at the park was talking about the grumpy old man because of how he treated guests. The toilets were outdated, parking was tight and inconvenient, and overall the staff were horrible. The place had a lot of potential, but unfortunately, the people running it really let it down. Wouldn’t recommend it based on our experience.

Stayed 5 nights with family, had a deluxe cabin,standard cabin and powered site, deluxe cabin was excellent spacious with good amenities, standard cabin was good a bit outdated but comfortable for the price, only complaint with the standard cabin was the double bed matress was very uncomfortable, powered site was good if not a bit squeezey if you were to have a big set-up. Grass was excellent and well presented. I will return to this caravan park, an enjoyable time was had. Very close to beach

The staff took all my food from the fridge in the kitchen and threw it in the bin while we were still there. The toilets had no soap and the stove tops needed replacing.  the park needs to be bulldozed and replaced.  The new owners or caretakers don't want to spend any money .
I am my friends will never come back here again

Cabin was nice
Location awesome
Management not so great,  definitely have to work on their PR skills
We were there as a group and not first time group booked this site but we definitely won't be back nor be recommending. One person was charged for extra bed linen. We nearly got charged an extra amount for the extra guests in our cabin. Good thing we are on the ball . Went to return key yesterday morning,  lady in office saw us coming, quickly went inside and stuck office unattended sign up  on the outside,  and locked office. Male manager was cleaning cabins,  came to ours as we were almost ready to leave,  we asked if wanted us to move our vehicle.  Abruptly  said "yeah would be a good idea" and stormed off up the ramp to clean cabin. We were even scolded my lady manager on Saturday night for being "too loud" told us to pipe down...quite rude in way went about it. Besides poor management/PR  company was great.  Did notice a for sale sign so I wonder if current management just don't really care anymore. Room for vehicles not the best either and lighting at night time could be heaps better too
We had a deluxe cabin, close to beach, short walk town. Nice and quiet. It would of been nice if there was milk in fridge when we arrived, seen there was coffee/tea supplied.
Always a wonderful stay in this town and caravan park.
Very nice!

Magnificent caravan park, lovely staff and spotless facilities. We enjoyed this place so much, started at 3 nights left after 9 nights. It has a cosy and friendly atmosphere. Couldn't recommend highly enough. Ps had a small issue with my 2nd battery, went to Port Vincent Fuels Mechanical Tyre Centre and the guy there was fantastic. Great to see old fashion service, thank you.

Efficient and friendly checkin. Clean and tidy camp sites. Plenty of clean showers and toilets.
Walking distance to the few shops, pub and kiosk.
Caravan sites are well grassed with good pads and good size.
Stayed there during the Adl Cup weekend. Great caravan Park. Very clean and the staff were helpful and friendly. My friend booked for us at site 41.
Great caravan park. Clean and tidy. Visited my Dad who had an onsite spot.
Its full and tight. Its also the best part of summer. Just enjoy it.

Fantastic friendly caravan park, nice clean amenities, pet friendly, beautiful bay for swimming, kayaking paddleboarding and fishing, a kiosk nearby for your daily coffee, fresh donuts, fish n chips and much more overlooking the bay 👌
Powered site Beach view just lovely 😍
Clean facilities, very friendly and helful staff, large caravan site. Super close to town beach

This is a popular caravan park in the Summer Months. Great little town, crabs are easy to get when they are in season and you can even catch Whiting off the beach on the high tides.
We booked a week in November 2025 on a powered site for  March 2026 and was looking forward to all of those activities.  A deposit was paid via debit card.
Unfortunately owing to a down turn in my health and advice from my GP we cancelled the booking in January, still leaving plenty of time for the site to be rebooked.
This is when all the drama and craziness began.
Firstly the booking was cancelled via e-mail.  After a week having heard nothing back from the Park I rang them. I had a conversation with the Office Lady who checked the records and found yes the booking had been cancelled.
I then asked about the refund of the deposit. I already knew there a fee for the cancellation.
I was told they could not refund from the Caravan Park and I would have to contact the Yorke Peninsula Council and arrange for the refund.
So I got all the paper work and set about filling it out.
Even though they already had my full details including address and phone number they wanted it all again.
I had to give a reason for the cancellation which I think is unwarranted but not to cause hassles I simply put medical.
Then they wanted a photocopy of my Bank Account including name and account number, but I was allowed to black out the balance in the account.
I thought this was very strange and instead of that I did a copy and paste of the transaction itself.
Mind you they already had all these details when the booking was made and deposit was paid.
For security reasons there was no way  I was going to comply with that request. It was a ridiculous request to me made and made me shake my head.
All I can say is that this is some scheme devised to make getting a refund just about impossible.
I have been travelling Oz with my Van since 2007. We have stayed at 100’s of Parks and have cancelled several during that time frame.
All of them simply credited back to my account.

This is a popular caravan park in the Summer Months. Great little town, crabs are easy to get when they are in season and you can even catch Whiting off the beach on the high tides.|We booked a week in November 2025 on a powered site for  March 2026 and was looking forward to all of those activities. It was a ridiculous request to me made and made me shake my head.|All I can say is that this is some scheme devised to make getting a refund just about impossible.|I have been travelling Oz with my Van since 2007. We have stayed at 100’s of Parks and have cancelled several during that time frame.|All of them simply credited back to my account.

One of our favourite get aways in South Australia, such a beautiful place.
Staff are friendly, get service and always keep the facilities clean every day.

Up for sale
Overpriced for room
Manager didn't want to be there.

The owner/manager of this establishment is one of the most objectionable, mean-spirited individuals I have met in a very long time. He seems to take pleasure in being rude to people and runs the park more like a prison camp than a family park. Friends of ours had him banging on their cabin door after they had gone to bed one night, demanding they move their vehicle. I also saw him berating some of the children who were innocently riding their push bikes around hurting nobody. He seems to have issues dealing with people, and rides his Harley Davidson motorcycle out of the park at a great rate of knots with a great deal of noise!
The park is up for sale ( I believe it was an inheritance), and I am sure he just wants to offload it, grab the money and run. He certainly does not have the skills, time or temperament to run anything to do with dealing with the public.
I spoke to local businesses about him and there seems to be a common theme about the frustration they feel with the image he is presenting of the town.
My suggestion is, don't stay here, it is not an enjoyable experience. Spend your hard earned money elsewhere. Do yourself a favour and head to the Port Vincent Foreshore Caravan Park.

Response to Managements Reply:

Our club is a classic motorcycle club restoring and riding classic British bikes. Our average age is 70+. members of the local Yorke  Peninsula community are members of our club.

Burnouts, I don't think so!

Over the week end we support the local CWA, the Bowls Club, the Hardwick Bay Progress Association and the Coast Watch. The interaction with them was great.  I am sure they would be happy to have us visit them again.

I think you are struggling here to condone your poor, rude customer service.

We stayed for 4 nights with friends, we had our caravans. The designated places were too small to fit our cars and have our awning out for comfort and socialising. We parked nearby in a couple of vacant places as there appeared to be no one else staying in the park at that time. The grumpy man (owner) walked past, didn't greet us, and told us to move the cars onto the road (outside the park grounds) because someone might come. I wondered if we could move to another spot, so went to ask at reception if we could park in an unoccupied car park and we would be happy to move the cars if needed. The lady wasn't friendly either, she said "they're the rules", and she didn't have time to come and ask us to move cars.
We moved our cars, no body came that night. All other spots were vacant.
Next day it felt uncomfortable having the owners walk up and down for most of the day, cleaning cabins. No "hello" or "good morning ", no smiles. They've definitely been in this job too long. Won't be coming back to this caravan park!
The beach was great and close. Rather than extend our stay, we moved to another park on the peninsula.
Note to self....   next time, read the reviews.

Location is close to the ocean but away from the main part of town. The accommodation is clean and tidy, however there is little in the way of additional facilities and even the mini golf is $10 per person to play.

Booked a deluxe cabin. Very clean, comfy, with everything we need. A kitchen is perfect for a stay. Unfortunately, unattended office on Sunday afternoon so we could not rent the rake for crabbing. Definitely will book again.
Loved our time here well cared for grounds and facilities

The wildlife is sooooo cooool!!
Cute rabbits, pelicans, and Seabirds abound.

WOW! What a lovely, quiet caravan park. Absolutly, beautiful spot to spend a few days. The caravan park is spotlessly clean and I had a lovely, comfortable cabin with a view of the ocean. I found the owners were very friendly and helpfull and I will definatly be back again.
Very nice quiet small park.

Went there Easter weekend.what a shambles.the office was closed all weekend who on the busiest weekend of the year closes thier office for the entire weekend.the cabin had the bare minimum in it had to provide my own bedding the bed was as hard as a rock. And to cap it off they held an auction on the Saturday to try to sell the dump.What a waste of a good weekend never going back there again.Gave 1star cos I can't give 0

I have just spent a week at this beautiful caravan park and I think it is the best caravan park on the Yorke Peninsula. There was great fishing and crabbing straight out the front and an easy walk in the town shops and pub. It is a lovely quiet, beachfront park with helpful and friendly owners/managers and it had the greenest, manicured grass sites I have ever seen. It is a credit to the owners!

Have just spent 6 wonderful weeks on Yorke Peninsula where towns and caravan parks are very close together.. Arrived at this park at 12.30 and was told no access to site until 2pm. as sprinkler was on site
Whilst this is broadly typical of most parks I would have thought not unreasonably that as you need to be off site by 10.00 enough time had passed for watering. Have not encountered this problem anywhere else on the Peninsula. Sites very small with choice of awning out and car on the street or car in and no awning. Very grumpy man who should not be dealing with the public. Would not return
A bit tired and old but clean.

My wife and I recently stayed at this park. We parked where directed by Sharon but unfortunatly our caravan was not parked in the correct area. One day later, Mark called out as he rode past that the caravan was to be shifted. Communication between Sharon and Mark would have solved this problem. Mark did not stop to speak to us but called out as he was driving. He was rude, showed  he has no interpersonal skills whatsoever which are required when running a business. He has an aggressive personality and seems to enjoy conflict. The kitchen area is to be avoided if considering cooking any meals. The  refrigerator has a strong smell coming from inside. I have stayed here several times and thoroughly enjoyed the park but encountering Mark always puts a dampener on the visit. Until the park is  sold I will not be returning.
If you inadvertently do something which does not suit Mark you will encounter his bad manners and temper.  Best advice is to give this park a miss, plenty more caravan locations on the penninsular. I fully expect Sharon to write a half sarcastic reply to this review but she would be better spending her time speaking to Mark about customer relations and customer satisfaction. This park runs  much better when Mark and Sharon employed managers and they stay away.
Management not very welcoming or helpful.

Absolutely disgusting management, do not know how to make you feel welcome, and mannerism is non existent, sites are very small with nowhere to park.|Will never stay here again and will strongly advise everyone to not stay here.|Travelled all over Australia and have never been spoken to by any other manager like we were spoken too by this man, he is a bully and should not be in charge of this park.

Stayed several nights in one of the deluxe cabins. The place looked tired and dated but it was their customer service which really let them down.

The "old grumpy" man referred to in prior one star reviews (from 4 yrs ago) is still there and he is still rude and aggressive. Surprised management lets him get away with his bad attitude towards customers and makes excuses for him.  He really should not be in the tourism industry if he is unable to communicate in a respectful or courteous manner.  Interactions with him are unpleasant and really puts a dampener on the holiday.

Pics attached.
Electric BBQ - set aside plenty of time if you're cooking for more than 4 ppl. Takes forever to warm up and the highest setting is just barely hot.
Communal kitchen - small with no ventilation. Two sinks but one doesn't even have water running to it.
Playground - old swing and one slide without adequate ground cover should your child fall.  Even if they don't advertise having a playground, the mere fact that it is on their premises, they should ensure it is up to standard.
Activity room - it is an old classroom in a former school. These premises are not secured so anyone could enter/exit and with many empty rooms and hiding spots.  Safety was a concern, even though I was watching my children the whole time.

Cabins - only 2 small pots and 1 frying pan with a faulty handle. Not enough to cook a family meal. Bed linen is old and worn. Upon checking in, the floor was already dusty and sandy, as if it had not been mopped properly.

Tried to book at the Foreshore Caravan Park, unfortunately it was booked out.  After reading all the reviews, I was really reluctant to book here, but I thought I would give them the benefit of the doubt.  I should have gone with my instincts and steered clear away from this caravan park. I will not stay here again and would strongly advise everyone else to think twice before booking here.
We have stayed here for the last 3 years and have always had a lovely time. The park is always well maintained and clean.
Staff were very rude and cabins were outdated and not clean.

Having decided to try our hand at catching blue crabs, we stayed at the park. We were fortunate enough to reserve a beach front cabin. The cabin was in great condition, very clean and well priced. Staff were friendly. Great place for any family if you enjoy a beach for crabbing with warm water.

Working facilities, had an unpowered grassy spot ;-)
quite outdated  camp kitchen ( need proper ventilation ) the fridge was more like a freezer and a bit smelly :-/ and the second small toilets need probably better ventilation too or canalization ... :-/ 😅
3,5 ***
Overall- 1 night unpowered site 25$ good ;-) happy

I wasn't even a guest here and thank god, my friends and I are travelling through and decided to stay in Port Vincent the night. They paid for a spot and I had parked on the road outside the park. I walked over to have lunch with my friends and was so rudely confronted by the grumpy old owner. Told me " nah that won't work, I'll just give you guys a refund and you can all piss off". I expressed I was having lunch with my friends and moving on for the night, to which he just scowled at us all and shook his head. For the next 10mins of the stay he scowled us. Requested a refund and left and thankgod cause we found way better customer service and views at Marion bay caravan park.
Staff at the desk were lovely, prays and thoughts to you both working there. Blink twice for refuge.

First of all no bedding and dirty room with hair all over the pillows!! Upon arrival.

The game machines just take your money.. with old chocolates in them that you cant eat!! (Dont waste your money) Text the number to get my money back (service guy) and he didn't refund me. (Pool table)

The free BBQ's (don't use these) they can take 1 hour to cook. They want your money so just pay to use the BBQ (hopefully it works)

Used the mini golf 'small fee may apply' the lady charged me $50!! without being explained this isnt refundable, the course is broken! And need maintenance.

Very dissapointing.
Great spot right on the water .Well looked after. 👍 for amenities

This is a great caravan park. The location is stunning, right on the waters edge, close to town, and with a great view over the St Vincent Gulf. The whole park is very tidy, and the amenities spotless. Watching the sun rising over a distant Adelaide is special.
Beautiful setting and cabins were lovely and clean
Great little park right on the beach.

Great caravan park on the beach.. Reception put herself out and very friendly. Very clean amenities and well set out. Lots of walks along the beach. Eating places not far away. Our dogs loved chasing the rabbits. Everywhere
Great cabin and location
Great place to stay with plenty of room in deluxe cabins and great ocean views
Stayed on one of your powered sites and have nothing but positive to say about our stay great little park and we will be back for sure
The place is quiet, clean, organised, has a sea view and the location is good to explore Yorke peninsula.

Good location
Not a lot of beach front sights
Clean and tidy
Overall
Andrew james 😊
Clean and attractive. Good amenities.  Good price
Great spot
This is a gorgeous caravan park. Beautifully maintained with immaculate facilities.
Awesome as always.
Great spot

Such a spectacular location. Great site positions; sheltered from the winds by the cabins which overlook the southern ocean. Very clean and well maintained. A great playground area for children. Within walking distance to the town centre. Definitely look forward to a return visit.
This is a very nice caravan park we love staying in those little cabins near the beach and look at the sunrise and sunset and the best fish and chips near the park.

We went in winter so the weather was against us but we had a great short break. Took the dog and had one of the two dog friendly cabins which was right by the sea. The rabbits made me laugh as we walked around the park, very unusual but I’m sure visitors with kids would have a laugh. Staff were great park was great our view was excellent 👍

Friendly place , beautiful location, amenities spotless and WiFi...
Showers one of the nicest in Australian van parks we have experienced.
Great

We parked our van here for a week. Did day trips around the area from here. Town is great. Pub, several cafes, great little fish shop, an IGA, and a great 4km round walk from here to the jetty and back. The owners are very friendly and helpful, amenities are very clean as is the laundry facilities,and would recommend this park no hesitation.
Right on the water. Highly recommend.
Lovely caravan park & beautiful spot to visit
Recently had a powered sites. Really impressed with how tidy and clean the amenities were. Park was kept clean , well kept bins, lawns. Beautiful views . Can't wait to come back 😁

Beautiful clean park
Staff friendly at the front
Concert steps near water where you can sit and enjoy watching family swim.
Bring life jackets for little ones as it can become deep.
Walk to shops, pier, pub & ice cream take away shop on the beach.
Sunrise are beautiful
There is a concert pier at the park where you can fish off.
Lots of Pelicans they are friendly.
Has a kids playground in caravan park.
Clean amenities, blowy location.

We came and spent 2 nights. Totally amazing back in Dec of 2022||We decided to try again when coming back to Australia a year later - which is in November and December. |We tried calling and emailing. No response. Decided to book online hoping for a response. Had to reach out many times again to confirm if it was available. Then once we booked - found out we could get there so needed to cancel. Terrible time trying to obtain a response again!!! And I still have not heard anything. Love the place. But due to lack of response won’t go back again. And won’t recommend this.
This park was established in 1942 according to the sign.  It's in a nice spot, but not on the "front" beach, around the corner..  well run and maintained.  Pretty quiet.  Great place.
Just a small caravan Park with cabins. All looks fairly new and well maintained. Ablution blocks are older but very clean. We are in a caravan

Loved this little park. Older park with new facilities be added all the time. Very quiet park, lots of bird life and lovely green sites to park on. They have their ideas how to protect their sites and good on them for doing this. Would stay them again.

What a fabulous park this is, waaaay more sheltered from the wind/breeze then the headland c/van park in town. We got a ocean front spot (drive through) so even if weather dicey, you can see waterfront out frontwindow. Tiny walk along ocean to town.  Absolutely wonderful showers for the women. Would highly recommend this place. Laundry $4.20. And site in sept $38. Brilliant.

Stayed in our caravan for our mini break here. Julie welcomed us warmly and we picked our site, beachfront of course. Amenities were great and very clean. The grounds within the park were well maintained and lush. The park managers and the grounds man were friendly and helpful.
Big tick from us, spent  a peaceful enjoyable few days. Thank you
Very accomodating we only needed one night and they were more than happy to oblige. Highly recommend.

This is a bloody great holiday spot very accommodating and alot to do plus they have brought extra land and buildings to make the most of like a games room basketball court camp kitchen and more the list goes on and they have a great mini putt putt course on the beach front 😀
I proudly promote my bespoke tourist groups to stay here when on YP. Cheers Jeff Teague Owner/Operator of Teague Tours 😊🇦🇺👍
Amazing rooms… had a great experience

Staff are always friendly. Our spa cabin was clean and comfortable. we had everything we needed. The veiw is amazing bird life everywhere. Close to a kiosk little supermarket and pub. It's just perfect. We can recommend it . We were able to take our greyhound with us, there is a fee but we didn't mind paying. Will be back soon.

Absolutely beautiful  we stayed in an ensuite cabin.  The cabin was clean and tidy, it contained everything we needed and more. The view is so beautiful. Thank you to the Manager Kyle for making our  stay so pleasant 😊. 10/10.

Great powered sites for camper, right on tge beach front, super clean amenities. Walking distance to Pirt Vincent Town.  Very happy, great place to chill or go fishing or pleasant walks.  Dogs allowed too 🙂😄

Fantastic little park in a perfect location. Only downside I would say is that the long term stays makes it really hard to get a foreshore site. But love the park and will always recommend it to people.

Perfect spot to wind down and relax.
Quite windy at this time of year.

Port Vincent Foreshore Caravan Park is situated on a Prominent Beach Point overlooking Sandy Beach on the York Peninsula that is very Tidal. Ebbing Tide gives Visitors a chance to catch some Blue Swimmer 🦀 Crabs and check out the Rock Pools. Port Vincent Foreshore Caravan Park provides a Fish Cleaning Station with Sinks, Boards and Water to clean your CATCH 🎏🎣🐟🐠 The Park has a large Shower Area, Laundry Facilities and Clotheslines, BBQ Area, Playground, Meeting Room and lots MORE. Boards...Bikes and Boats/Canoes can be HIRED. Lawn and Grassed Sites for Caravans and Tents, plus many Modern Cabins Available. Book a Site because this Caravan Park is POPULAR.🤗
Unhappy stay, stayed last three nights and were forced to park on a over watered and muddy site, and only changed another site after negotiations on the 2nd day, not happy.

The staff of the caravan park had a bad attitude. We booked three sites and two for motorhome and campervan, one for caravan. There are some muds in caravan site and hard to parked there so we offered caravan to park beside us, because motorhome and campervan only occupied one and half site.  The staff came over with bad attitude told us not allowed to park beside us also locked us out at night and force us to move the caravan tomorrow otherwise we will be kick out even we had one more night to stay. We know someone who are racially discriminatory was going to report and staff came over with bad attitude to order us to do the things. We have been in Australia for 35 years. It is first time to meet the things like this. Shame on these people!
Fabulous
The receptionist was very rude.... never ever experienced such a rude srvice...

when I first got there the manager want not very welcoming that day. when we entered the cabin, I accidently dried water on the floor so I wiped it up and the floor was duty! as soon as we entered - I think they only sweep the floor not washing the floor.
the quilt was duty and I was going to use that to keep warm! the day we left we signed out and the manager was grumpy not happy bcs we told her what happened at our stay.
Never stayed, never will

Perfect little caravan park with every van bay and cabin having beautiful views over the coast. The staff were ultra responsive and ultra helpful. A place of peace that I would recommend for families and workers. Walking distance to pub and takeaway. Quiet.

One of our fav caravan parks in Oz
We keep going back and so lucky to get a foreshore powered site   The most beautiful views
Kyle and his wife just lovely and nothing is a problem
The staff again so very helpful and can't do enough to make you welcome
Amenities spotless
Highly recommend this wonderful park
We will be back again and again
Annie & Phil xxx

Cabins old and lack insulation. Cobwebs all over balcony. Cabin kitchen utensils not sharp and not many of them, eg no scissors.
Camp kitchen has no utensils.

Bed comfortable and great showers.

Our experience was fantastic, friendly service by Cara and Kyle, the park is incredible, clean and has all of the amenities required. Great views of the water. We would definitely return. Thank you for a wonderful stay

We wanted to get away before Christmas
Not too far from home was on the radar

So off from suburbia and heading to Yorke Peninsula we went

Childhood memories were for another town on the Pen

Arriving at Port Vincent on a full Friday we’d hoped we could get a powered site

We were accommodated well and were quite happy to not be beach front …been there, done that, before

What a great park …clean, tidy, well appointed and with spotless ammenities might I add too
All very well looked after by the resident managers

Being here a week at the beginning of November we got to stay and pay for 6 and get the seventh night free …perfect

As the Melbourne Cup stops the nation all the travellers in the park were invited to a free bbq lunch Cup sweeps and some fun stuff …a quiz and a hobby horse race just to pass the time
All they asked was we bring a salad to share …easy done

As we’re only about 200kms from home it will certainly be added to out ‘must stay at again’ list

Thank you for such a great stay

We stayed at this caravan park for 3 nights in our caravan on a water front site and it was amazing. Views were just beautiful and our kids loved being able to walk straight from the caravan down to the beach. Amenities and park was very clean.

Will definitely visit again. Thank you again.
Great spot, the only way to make it better would be to have free beer,

Omg wow this has to be the best place we’ve stayed   Great location ( we did have water front site ) we were fishing from the back of our van   Easy walk to the IGA and a little walk to the best donuts and other yummies at the kiosk   Amenities are kept lovely and clean , everyone is gorgeous. Just love this place
Great little park. Would stay again.

Fabulous beach front views.  Neat, clean little cabin, with everything we needed.

Thanks for a great stay.  See you next time.

(We had an ensuite cabin).
Very nice caravan park,right on the ocean, clean facilities.

Quick trip to Port Vincent to get away and stayed here. The amenities were spotless, the park well maintained and it's a close walk to all that's in Port Vincent. It wasn't busy but I can imagine when the weather gets a bit warmer this place would be a superb place to relax. Congrats to the managers for a gem of a place to stay

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Our foreshore site was easily accessible. The views of the Bay were outstanding and the sounds of the waves at night made for a restful sleep.
The facilities are all top notch and clean. A credit to the manager and staff.
We will definitely be recommending this caravan park to friends and family.

We stayed on the beach front in a powered site, payed for 5 stay 7 we ended up staying 9 nights.
In those 9 nights a long weekend came up We were not charged any extra.
The park was 90% full over the long weekend yet park and BOTH toilet facilities were kept spotless.
Kylie (park manager) works like a machine always doing something around the park.
There is many BBQ facilities available, big TV room, camp kitchen spotless.
There's a gas fire pit, big screen TV with plenty of space to watch late night sport.
Fish cleaning, boat engine wash.
Short walk to corner shops, pub.

Port Vincent,  if you want to leave the world behind,  it's the best 2 hour drive from Adelaide you could ever do. And the foreshore caravan park is pure joy, The caravan sites are large and the cabins over look the gulf.  Do yourself and the family a favour and go for a weekend  or a week.

A wonderful little caravan park next to the beach, the kids loved it, the amenities very clean.
Amazing spot we have books to stay here again
One of the best caravan parks around.. great location too
Very nice place to stay.
Awesome little park close to town. We had a site backing onto the foreshore. Stunning sunsets and very peaceful watching the dolphins go by. Clean amenities and camp kitchen

Nice caravan park, good location.
I will talk to anyone, the locals were the same, just found staff in some places not really interested...maybe it's been a long tourist season. In the area food needs some more variety, easy said I know but that's how we found it. Will go back but will take more provisions from home.

Great little caravan park right on the water. Playground is more for bigger kids but there’s another within walking distance next to the kiosk which has great donuts and coffee. Clean toilets. We enjoyed our stay and will likely be back
Our RV club stays at various parks across SA, and I find this park is the best by far in all respects. Also, the management is the easiest to deal with.

Not impressed. Called ahead to made sure they had unpowered sites for us as been travelling for 7 hrs with a caravan. Arrived to be told oh no you have to pay a powered sites as no unpowered left.her words were oh i thought you wanted powered site and was expecting us to still stay. So we rang up Edithburgh caravan park and Daniel organised a spot while we were still on the phone travelling and I'm glad we found this place.its beautiful and we stayed longer then was going to too.

What a gem!! Right on the water with beautiful views and fantastic facilities. We loved the giant tv and the gas fireplace. The manager, Kyle, does a brilliant job at attending to all the cleaning and maintenance of the BBQ’s, bins and shower block. Everything was tickity boom!!

We stayed at Port Vincent foreshore Caravan Park 3x  Beutiful location Only downside was there wasn't any air-conditioning in our Bedroom and spare bedroom   it was unbaringly hot in our bedroom  we had to go and buy ourselves a Desk fan  to keep us cool. There was an air-conditioning in the lounge room only   something staff  need to look into  if guests are going to stay

We had the  big Blue Villa in the park...very roomy everything supplied in Villa fabulous location in park...it was as tho we were on an island with no other people... beautiful views and quiet ...I would have liked a hand rail on the back stairs tho that's all I could suggest

One of the best parks I've been to.
Great facilities and location.
First time campers at this location. Amenities very clean. Staff very friendly and helpful. Great location. Definitely looking to return
Great sites, very neat and tidy. Amazing service and lovely people. Highly recommend.
Absolutely brilliant we love coming here,  staff are warm and friendly park is in tip top shape, amenities are super clean

To be closer to the shoreline, you would have to be floating.

Brilliant views from the cabin.

Highly recommend
Just visiting friends who were staying in the park.
Clean, neat,lots of grass sites . Right on the sea and a reasonable price. Amenities good

This is a fantastic caravan park.
Great camp kitchen, with a fully enclosed dining area.
Got a few crabs at low tide.
Nice beach. Just up the road is a fish n chip shop, and across the road is a hotel.
At short notice, managed to get a powered beach front site. Staff were very friendly, check-in was easy and facilities were very clean. Could not fault anything.
Beautiful views

We were absolutely pleased with the position of the cabin ( cabin 8 ) the layout and cleanliness , Beds where comfortable .Staff made everything from booking to returning the key a pleasurable experience will absolutely return . Waterfront cabins

Ok...what can I say ?? The cabin was immaculate, and I had to literally walk 10 steps at high tide to go beach fishing. The staff ??? Bloody incredible...couldnt do enough to help and always with a smile. The amenities....top of the range. The views...to die for !!!! Absolutely is forever my " to go to " place now. I dont need to travel Australia. I found this little fairytale place instead. Thank you to everyone associated with that Park....Cannot wait to return. Xxx
Great place to stay cabins clean park beautiful maintained staff are friendly and scenery was beautiful Didn't really want to leave..This is from a queenlander Judy B
Great location. We got a site against the sea bank. We bought fish n’ chips from the kiosk nearby and just sat at the bank enjoying the food, watching the peaceful ocean and basking in the sun.
A beautiful place. We will definitely be back.

Just enjoyed 4 days on a powered site ,ablutions were spotlessly clean,and grounds were incredibly well kept, lawn in great condition with edges trimmed.A very pleasant stay ,with friendly staff a big thank you to Kyle for lending me a socket and ratchet to fix my anode in caravan hot water service,Would highly recommend am returning next month for another peaceful stay.

If you're ever wanting to get away this is the place to go the people are brilliant and very helpful the park grounds are maintained beautifully
We stayed in one of the cabins very affordable very well looked after
There is plenty to do for the kids and for the grown-ups
Only a stone throw from the main street
Couldn't get any closer to sea front. Very well kept. Should of booked longer stay. Didn't want to go home.

Stayed in cabin 4 which was very nice. Balcony facing beach with great views. Queen bed was perfect cabin very clean @ all other areas very pleasing.  Check in was easy @would stay here again no problem. Thanks for a nice stay.
Stayed in Deluxe Cabin. Clean & tidy. Great view of ocean. Staff very friendly.
Very beautiful. Definitely go back again
The cleanest caravan park I have ever stayed in. Management was great and great service
Warm and friendly reception spacious park and great amenities fantastic camp kitchen nice outside area with gas log fire and outside big tv

We love Port Vincent beautiful clean facilities.
The best fish and chips at the kiosk.
Cabin stay was great.  Very clean and tidy. Supplied linen and blankets, but in cooler months, take more to keep warm. Also towels supplied.   Great views,  great park facilities.
Super clean throughout, great set up. Loads for the kids to do. Awesome location. Would recommend highly. AAA+++

Quiet and peaceful, very welcoming.
Beach front cabins amazing, clean and roomy.
